content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-enabled autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which unlabeled data gets written to an active learning agent's long-term memory, solving the 2024-2025 SOTA challenge of catastrophic forgetting and memory bloat in open-ended active learning agents. Instead of only using task prediction uncertainty to select new training samples, agents use intrinsic curiosity reward to gate memory retention: high-curiosity unexpected samples are stored for future learning, while redundant low-curiosity samples are discarded.
